subject: Product Management
title: Fear of Change
description: Learn Fear of Change in one minute.

hook.text: When Slack replaced email at a company, some employees rebelled. They'd used email for 20 years. Change felt threatening.

buildup.text: Change resistance isn't about the tool.

discovery.text: The Kübler-Ross change curve applies to product adoption: denial, anger, bargaining, depression, acceptance.

twist.text: Forcing adoption without addressing emotional resistance creates shadow IT — users secretly continue using the old tool.

climax.text: Successful adoption involves users early, gives them control over the transition, and celebrates early wins to build momentum.

punchline.text: Change the tool, but respect the human. Adoption is emotional, not just logical.

quiz.question: Why do people resist adopting new tools even when they're objectively better?
options: Change resistance is about identity and comfort — it's emotional, not just logical
People always prefer the newest tools available
Resistance only happens with poorly designed products
